core_tone.name: Engineering Authority Voice
core_tone.definition: A precise, structured, and technically grounded communication style that prioritizes clarity, logic, and system thinking over emotional persuasion.
core_tone.essence: We communicate like engineers, not advertisers

tone_principles.description: Messages must be immediately understandable and technically unambiguous.
Communication must focus on systems, not isolated products.
Arguments are based on engineering reasoning, not emotional appeal.
No superlatives or vague marketing claims are allowed.

formality.description: Professional and structured communication suitable for engineers and EPC stakeholders

technical_depth.description: Uses engineering logic and system-based explanations

emotionality.description: Minimal emotional language; controlled and neutral tone

dont: Use decorative storytelling without engineering logic

architectural.style: Balanced between design clarity and engineering logic

executive.use_case: Business communication with EPCs and stakeholders

brand_voice_identity.communication_role: Translator between architecture and execution systems
brand_voice_identity.core_message_style: We explain systems, not sell products

strategic_goal.objective: Establish AvizSazeh as a trusted engineering voice in architectural systems
strategic_goal.impact: Ensure all communications reinforce system credibility and execution reliability
